remediations.risk: Executes arbitrary code from a string. If user-controlled, full code execution.
remediations.fix: Replace eval() with a safe parser. For math: use ast.literal_eval() or a dedicated expression library. For JSON: use json.loads(). Never pass user input to eval().
remediations.example:
# UNSAFE
result = eval(user_input)

# SAFE
import ast
result = ast.literal_eval(user_input)  # raises on non-literals

remediations.risk: Executes arbitrary code from a string. If user-controlled, full code execution.
remediations.fix: Replace eval() with a safe parser. For math: use ast.literal_eval() or a dedicated expression library. For JSON: use json.loads(). Never pass user input to eval().
remediations.example:
# UNSAFE
result = eval(user_input)

# SAFE
import ast
result = ast.literal_eval(user_input)  # raises on non-literals

remediations.risk: Dynamically constructs and executes a function from a string. Equivalent to eval().
remediations.fix: Eliminate dynamic function construction entirely. Use a lookup table (object/map) to map user input to pre-defined safe functions.

remediations.risk: Synchronous shell command. Blocks event loop and enables command injection.
remediations.fix: Use child_process.execFile() with an explicit args array. Sanitize all inputs with a whitelist before passing to any exec function.

remediations.risk: Deferred code execution via string argument. eval() equivalent on a timer.
remediations.fix: Always pass a function reference, never a string, to setTimeout.
